title: PHYSECON
PHYSECON
PHYSECON
text:
2 Potential
\(\Rightarrow\) Wave equation that determines Potential: \[\begin{split} &
\Delta \cdot U - \frac{1}{c^2} \frac{\partial^2}{\partial t^2} U = J
\end{split} \tag{6}\]
\(\Rightarrow\) Potential caused by “ignition J” (inhomgenity) from source at rest, v = 0: \[\begin{split} &
U = -\frac{1}{4\pi} \int \frac{J}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\end{split} \tag{7}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
3 elec. Potential structure:
\(J = -\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \varrho\) \[\begin{split} &
U_e \coloneqq \Phi
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. charge dens.=}\varrho}{=}
\frac{1}{4\pi} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} \int \frac{\varrho}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} Q =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{\varepsilon_0} It
\end{split} \tag{8}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
4 magn. Potential structure:
\(J = -\mu_0 j\) \[\begin{split} &
U_m \coloneqq \boldsymbol{A}
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{elec. current dens.=}j}{=}
\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi} \int \frac{j}{\left | r  \right |} \, dr'
\newline &
\> \hat{=} \>
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \mu_0 I =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left | r \right |} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} I =
\frac{1}{c^2} \frac{1}{t} U_e
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{source in uniformal movement,} v = const. \neq 0}{=}
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \mu_0 Qv
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} vQ
\newline &
=
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Itv =
\frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\left( \left | r \right | - r\cdot\frac{v}{c} \right)} \frac{1}{c^2 \varepsilon_0} Ir
\end{split} \tag{9}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
5 Electric endogen energy
\(E_{pot} = mc^2\)
\[\begin{split} &
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
q = ne = \textnormal{charge} \qquad
n \in \mathbb{N}
\end{split} \tag{10}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\textnormal{Potential} \qquad
U =
\frac{E_{pot}}{q} =
\frac{\left( mc^2 \right)^2}{ne} =
\frac{m}{q} m c^4 =
\kappa m c^4
\end{split} \tag{11}\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
6 Electric induced energy (exogenous excitation)
\(E_{kin} = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \> \hat{=} \> \left( pc \right)^2 \> \hat{=} \> h\nu\)
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density electric: \[\begin{split} &
w_e = \frac{1}{2} \varepsilon_0 \mathscr{E}^2
\end{split} \tag{12}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Energy density magnetic: \[\begin{split} &
w_m = \frac{1}{2} \frac{1}{\mu_0} \mathscr{B}^2
\end{split} \tag{13}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Electric Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_e = \frac{1}{2} CU^2
\end{split} \tag{14}\]
\(E_{kin}\) Magnetic Energy: \[\begin{split} &
W_m = \frac{1}{2} LI^2
\end{split} \tag{15}\]
Power: \[\begin{split} &
P = VI = (U_1 - U_2) \frac{Q}{t} =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{t} = \frac{W}{t} = \frac{Fr}{t} = Fv
\end{split} \tag{16}\]

6.1 Conductor (charge conductive element for energy transfer) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\sigma \cdot t \gg \varepsilon_0
\end{split} \tag{17}\]


6.2 Slow changing current (quasi-stationary, low frequencies) condition:
\[\begin{split} &
\frac{v}{c} \ll \frac{1}{c}\frac{\mathscr{E}}{\mathscr{B}} \ll \frac{c}{v}
\end{split} \tag{18}\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
text:
7 Energy conservation for (large extended) conductive medium:
\[\begin{split} &   
\int    \left(
\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \left( we + w_m \right) + \mathbf{\nabla} \cdot \mathbf{\mathscr{S}} + \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E}
\right) \, dr
= \frac{d}{dt}W_e + \frac{d}{dt}W_m + D_S + \int \left( \boldsymbol{j} \cdot \mathscr{E} \right) \, dr
\newline &
\stackrel{\textnormal{low frequency}}{=}
\frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( LI^2 \right) + \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{dt} \left( \frac{Q^2}{C} \right) + \left( RI^2 - VI \right) = 0
\end{split} \tag{19}\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 8 Energy of thin conductor (concentrated conductive medium without electric charge in its surrounding), Voltage (potentialdifference, tension):

title: PHYSECON
text:
9 Low Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \ll \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \ll d \iff \rho \ll 1 \end{split} \tag{21}\] \[
L \approx const.    \qquad
C \approx const.    \qquad
D_S \approx 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
10 High Frequency
\[\begin{split} & \omega \gg \frac{1}{\mu \sigma} \frac{2}{r^2} \iff r \gg d \iff \rho \gg 1 \end{split} \tag{22}\] \[
L(t) \neq const.    \qquad
C(t) \neq const.   \qquad
\textnormal{for Dipol: } D_S \propto \omega^4 \gg 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
11 Continuity at Rest: Electic Charge
no Dissipation, no velocity, charge (ion) causes Electric Field

title: PHYSECON
text:
12 Continuity at Movement: Magnetic Field
no Dissipation, Velocity constant, moved charge (ion) causes emergence of Magnetic Field

title: PHYSECON
text:
13 Continuity at Acceleration: Radiation Energy
no Dissipation, Velocity changing, acceleration of charge (ion) causes emgergence of Radiation

title: PHYSECON
section: 14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
text:
14 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in VACUUM
\[
2\pi = k \lambda \gg kr_0 \ll 1
\]\[
\omega = kc \ll \frac{c}{r_0}     \qquad
\nabla \cdot j = i \omega \varrho     \qquad
i \omega Q = 0
\]

title: PHYSECON
section: 15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
text:
15 Continuity and periodic (harmoneous oscillation) Movement in MATTER
Refraction index emerges
Dispersion: \(k^2 = \varepsilon \mu \omega^2 + i \mu \sigma \omega\)

title: PHYSECON
text:
16 Near Field
\[
r \ll \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \ll 2\pi
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} \sim \frac{ \frac{\vec{p}^2}{\varepsilon_0 r^6} }{ \frac{k^2 \mu_0 c^2 \vec{p}^2}{r^4} } \sim \left( kr \right)^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\vec{p} = \textnormal{elec. Dipol}
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
17 Far Field
accelerated movement -> Radiation in Far Field of charge source
\[
r \gg \lambda  = \frac{2\pi}{k}
\stackrel{\textnormal{vacuum}}{=}
\frac{2\pi}{ \frac{\omega}{c} }
\qquad
kr \gg 1
\qquad
r \gg kr_0^2 \ll 1
\]
\[
\frac{w_m}{w_e} =
\frac{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 }{ \frac{\varepsilon_0}{2} c^2 \left( \mathscr{B} \times \frac{r}{\left| r \right|} \right)^2 } = 1
\]

title: PHYSECON
text:
18 Dissipation in Vacuum
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter, no Refraction index, Reflection

title: PHYSECON
text:
19 Dissipation in Matter
no Contiunuity of chrge/matter with Dielectricum, Refraction merges, Reflection, Absorbtion

title: PHYSECON
text:
20 Energy gain by excitation:
Action Quantum, Planck Constant (Energy-Frequency-Slope): 
\[\begin{split} &
h =
\tan(\alpha) =
\frac{E_{kin}}{\nu_i - \nu_0} =
\frac{eV_{0i}}{\nu_i - \nu_0}
\equiv \\&
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\nu_0} =
\frac{W}{\nu_0} =
\frac{F_{EM} \cdot x}{\nu_0} =
\hbar k \lambda
\end{split} \tag{23}\]
\[\begin{split} &
\hbar =
\frac{E_1 - E_2}{\omega} =
\end{split} \tag{24}\]
\[\begin{split} &
E_{kin}^{max} =
eV_0 =
e \frac{Q}{C} =
h(\nu - \nu_0)
\end{split} \tag{25}\]
\[
V_0 = \textnormal{Opposing Potential ($I=0$)}
\]\[
\alpha = \angle( E_{kin}, \nu ) \qquad
e = \textnormal{electron charge} \qquad
Q = \textnormal{ion charge} \qquad
\]\[
C = \textnormal{Capacitance} \qquad
\nu = \frac{1}{2\pi} \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}\sqrt{1 - \frac{C}{L}R^2}
\]\[
\Rightarrow \textnormal{Inductance } L = \frac{4\pi^2}{C} \left( \frac{e}{h}Q + \nu_0 \right)^2
\]


\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Ionisation Energy of chemical element } E_i = h\nu = h\frac{\lambda}{c} = \hbar\omega = \frac{h}{k\lambda} \omega\)
\(\Rightarrow \textnormal{Energy released (gained) } E_{gain} = E_{pot} - E_i = mc^2 - h\nu\)
